plant_constituents.name: phenolic acids
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa phenolic acids have been identified as a key component in the medicinal properties of black cohosh, a plant traditionally used in alternative medicine. These phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Research suggests that the phenolic acids in cimicifuga racemosa may contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic benefits in managing menopausal symptoms and other health conditions.

plant_constituents.name: flavonoids
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa flavonoids, a group of bioactive compounds, have been identified to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to the plant's potential therapeutic benefits. Research suggests that flavonoids in Cimicifuga racemosa may also exhibit neuroprotective effects, which could be beneficial in the management of neurodegenerative diseases. Additionally, these flavonoids may interact with other compounds in the plant to enhance its overall medicinal properties.

plant_constituents.name: tannins
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tannins have been identified as a key component in the medicinal properties of black cohosh, and studies suggest that they play a role in its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These polyphenolic compounds have been shown to contribute to the herb's ability to modulate the body's response to stress and promote overall health. Additionally, tannins may also have a role in protecting against oxidative damage and supporting the body's natural detoxification processes.

plant_constituents.name: sesquiterpenes
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa sesquiterpenes are a class of bioactive compounds found in the roots of the black cohosh plant, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. These sesquiterpenes have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to their potential therapeutic benefits. Research has also suggested that sesquiterpenes may play a role in modulating the body's hormonal balance, particularly in relation to menopausal symptoms.

plant_constituents.name: coumarins
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa coumarins, although present in small quantities, have been reported to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. However, it's essential to note that the primary medicinal properties of Cimicifuga racemosa are attributed to its triterpene saponins rather than coumarins. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the role and significance of coumarins in the medicinal properties of Cimicifuga racemosa.

plant_constituents.name: phenylpropanoids
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa phenylpropanoids are a class of bioactive compounds found in the roots of the black cohosh plant, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. These phenylpropanoids are believed to exert their medicinal effects by influencing the body's hormonal balance and reducing inflammation. Research suggests that specific phenylpropanoids, such as 23-epi-26-deoxycucurbitacins, may contribute to the therapeutic benefits associated with black cohosh.

plant_constituents.name: lignans
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa lignans, also known as secoiridoid glycosides, are a group of lignans found in the root of the Cimicifuga racemosa plant. These lignans have been studied for their potential therapeutic benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and may contribute to the plant's traditional use in treating menopausal symptoms. Research on Cimicifuga racemosa lignans continues to uncover their mechanisms of action and potential applications in modern medicine.

plant_constituents.name: caffeic acid
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa caffeic acid, a phenolic compound, has been identified as a key bioactive constituent in the plant. Research suggests that caffeic acid in Cimicifuga racemosa may possess antioxidant properties, helping to protect cells from oxidative damage. Additionally, caffeic acid has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

plant_constituents.name: rosmarinic acid
plant_constituents.desc:
Cimicifuga racemosa rosmarinic acid is not a known constituent of Cimicifuga racemosa. 
Cimicifuga racemosa is commonly known as black cohosh and is traditionally used to relieve symptoms of menopause. 
Phenolic compounds such as isoflavones and triterpene glycosides are found in Cimicifuga racemosa but rosmarinic acid is not associated with this plant.

plant_constituents.name: kaempferol
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa kaempferol is a flavonoid compound found in the root of black cohosh, a plant used in traditional medicine. Kaempferol has been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and estrogenic activity, which may contribute to its potential therapeutic effects. However, further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological properties and clinical applications of kaempferol in black cohosh.

plant_constituents.name: isorhapontigenin
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa isorhapontigenin is a triterpenoid saponin compound found in the roots of Cimicifuga racemosa, commonly known as black cohosh. Research on isorhapontigenin suggests it may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential therapeutic effects. However, further studies are needed to fully understand its effects and mechanisms of action in human health.

plant_constituents.name: actein
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa actein, a triterpene saponin, has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative effects. Research suggests that actein may inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis, making it a subject of interest in cancer treatment. However, further studies are needed to fully understand the mechanisms and therapeutic potential of actein.

plant_constituents.name: cimicifugoside
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa cimicifugoside, a saponin compound, is an active constituent found in black cohosh, traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Cimicifugoside has been reported to have estrogenic activity, potentially contributing to its use in treating menopausal symptoms. However, the efficacy and safety of cimicifugoside in this context require further research to confirm its benefits and potential side effects.

plant_constituents.name: deoxysphingosine
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa deoxysphingosine is a compound that has been isolated from the plant, however I couldn't find any information about it being a medicinal active constituent. I couldn't find any reliable sources confirming deoxysphingosine as a constituent of cimicifuga racemosa.

plant_constituents.name: cycloartanes
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa cycloartanes have been identified as a group of triterpenoid compounds with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. These compounds have been found in various plant extracts, including those obtained from Cimicifuga racemosa, and have been studied for their therapeutic applications. Research on cycloartanes is ongoing to fully understand their mechanisms of action and potential benefits in human health.

plant_constituents.name: sesquiterpene lactones
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa sesquiterpene lactones are not a well-documented or established class of constituents of the plant, and the majority of known constituents are triterpenoids, phenolic acids and glycosides. However, sesquiterpenes, such as cimicifugoside, have been identified in the plant, which exhibit biological activities. The exact classification and role of sesquiterpene lactones in Cimicifuga racemosa require further research and clarification.

plant_constituents.name: cimicifugic acid
plant_constituents.desc: Cimicifuga racemosa cimicifugic acid is a lignan, a type of phytoestrogen found in the roots of the black cohosh plant. It has been traditionally used to alleviate menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats, due to its potential to interact with estrogen receptors in the body. However, the scientific evidence supporting the efficacy of cimicifugic acid in treating menopausal symptoms is still limited and inconclusive.
